description:
You are a log-analysis specialist who has just received a new collection of system-event records in the file  

  /home/user/logs/system_events.log  

Your objectives are:

1. Automation  
   a. Write a shell script named /home/user/scripts/analyze_logs.sh (create the directory if it does not yet exist).  
   b. The script must:  
      • Read the entire /home/user/logs/system_events.log file.  
      • Count how many lines contain the strings “INFO”, “WARNING”, and “ERROR” (case-sensitive, exact substrings).  
      • Emit **one single-line summary** to **STDOUT** in the exact format shown below, substituting the live date-and-time of execution and the computed counters:  

        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS | INFO=<number> | WARNING=<number> | ERROR=<number>

        Example (the numbers below are only illustrative):  
        2023-08-31 14:07:05 | INFO=17 | WARNING=3 | ERROR=1  

   c. Make the script executable by the current user.

2. Scheduled execution  
   a. Register a cron job for **the current (non-root) user** that runs the script every 5 minutes.  
   b. The exact crontab entry must append all script output (stdout and stderr) to  

      /home/user/logs/analysis_cron.log  

      ensuring nothing else is executed in that job line.  
      Required pattern for the cron line (spacing must follow crontab rules):  

      */5 * * * * /home/user/scripts/analyze_logs.sh >> /home/user/logs/analysis_cron.log 2>&1

3. Immediate verification run  
   a. Manually execute the script once so that at least **one** summary line is already present in  

      /home/user/logs/analysis_summary.log  

      (create the file if it is absent).  
   b. After you finish, /home/user/logs/analysis_summary.log must contain **exactly one line** written by the manual run, matching the format in step 1.  
   c. Redirects in your manual command are up to you, but the final file path and its single-line content are mandatory.

4. Logging for the graders  
   a. Append the same single-line summary that you sent to analysis_summary.log to a second file called  

      /home/user/logs/analysis_task.log  

      This second file will be read by the automated tests; it, too, must contain exactly one line in the required format.

Important constraints & observable end-state that will be automatically graded:

• File /home/user/scripts/analyze_logs.sh exists and is executable.  
• The script prints the summary in the **exact** format described.  
• /home/user/logs/analysis_summary.log contains exactly one line; its INFO, WARNING, and ERROR totals must reflect the current contents of /home/user/logs/system_events.log.  
• /home/user/logs/analysis_task.log also contains that same single line.  
• Your personal crontab (shown by `crontab -l`) contains precisely one new job line, matching the pattern in step 2b verbatim.  
• The summary line’s counters will be verified: they must show the correct totals derived from the supplied system_events.log file, namely INFO=5, WARNING=2, ERROR=2.

Perform everything from a normal user shell; do not request sudo. When you are done, the grader will:

1. Confirm the script is executable.  
2. Execute the script once itself and check that it prints a correct summary line.  
3. Verify both analysis_summary.log and analysis_task.log contain identical single lines with the correct counts.  
4. Inspect your crontab for the exact `*/5 * * * * ...` entry.

You will know you are finished when the single-line summaries in both required files match the specified format and your crontab shows the scheduled job. No additional output files should be created.
truth:
Pre-existing artifacts (already on disk before the agent starts):

1. /home/user/logs (directory, 755)
2. /home/user/logs/system_events.log (file, 644) with exactly the nine lines below:

[2023-04-01 10:00:00] INFO User login: alice
[2023-04-01 10:05:00] WARNING Disk space low on /dev/sda1
[2023-04-01 10:07:24] ERROR Failed to connect to database
[2023-04-01 10:15:42] INFO Scheduled backup started
[2023-04-01 10:45:13] INFO Scheduled backup completed
[2023-04-01 11:00:00] ERROR Failed to send email
[2023-04-01 11:30:23] WARNING High memory usage detected
[2023-04-01 11:45:23] INFO User logout: alice
[2023-04-01 12:00:00] INFO User login: bob

Ground-truth counts inside that log:
INFO  = 5
WARNING = 2
ERROR   = 2

Artifacts that must exist when the agent finishes:

A. Script:
/home/user/scripts/analyze_logs.sh (755)
– Shebang: #!/bin/bash (exact)
– Must perform log counting as per task description and echo a single line exactly:
  $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') | INFO=5 | WARNING=2 | ERROR=2
  (with the live date substituted at runtime)

B. Cron job present in the user’s crontab:
*/5 * * * * /home/user/scripts/analyze_logs.sh >> /home/user/logs/analysis_cron.log 2>&1

C. Output files after agent work:
1. /home/user/logs/analysis_summary.log (640 or 644) – exactly one line, identical to script output (same timestamp) and containing “… | INFO=5 | WARNING=2 | ERROR=2”
2. /home/user/logs/analysis_task.log (640 or 644) – exactly one line, identical to the line above.

No other modifications to system_events.log are allowed.
